chapter_title.en: Chapter 29 – Obligation of (performing) the Hajj and the Umra

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/141)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/141)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْحَجُّ عَلَى الْغَنِيِّ وَ الْفَقِيرِ فَقَالَ الْحَجُّ عَلَى النَّاسِ جَمِيعاً كِبَارِهِمْ وَ صِغَارِهِمْ فَمَنْ كَانَ لَهُ عُذْرٌ عَذَرَهُ اللَّهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazan, altogether from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The Hajj is upon the rich and the poor?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The Hajj is upon the people altogether, their elders and their young ones. So the one who has an excuse for himself, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Excuse him’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Muhammad ibn ‘Isma‘il has narrated from af-Fadf ibn Shadhan from all from ibn abu ‘Umayr ‘Abd al- Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if al-Hajj is obligatory on both rich and poor people. He (the Imam) said, ‘Al-Hajj is obligatory on all people, grown up ones and small ones, only those who have good reasons Allah accepts their excuse.’”
